tipRules.tip: Johnston: Use a 3/4-ounce drop shot weight when targeting pressured smallmouth in deep water up to 35 feet on the St. Lawrence River.
Johnston: When fish aren't grouped up due to heavy pressure, adopt a run-and-gun approach hitting isolated structure rather than working one area. Hit 40+ spots catching one here and one there.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: When extreme fishing pressure pushes smallmouth off typical spots, hunt individual bass in areas with less pressure on flats with isolated structure.
Johnston: Use Garmin LiveScope to pick apart structure and look for bigger fish when smallmouth are pressured and not in their typical locations.
Johnston: Pressured St. Lawrence smallmouth can range from 2 feet to 35 feet deep in the same day. Be willing to fish the full water column.
Johnston: Heavy recreational and tournament pressure on the St. Lawrence causes fish to behave differently and not go to where they're typically at.
